text: 68% of Canadian grocery shoppers reported trading down to store-brand or private-label products in 2024 due to sustained inflation.
Canadian consumers in Ontario and Quebec show the highest price sensitivity for packaged foods, with 72% actively comparing unit prices across brands.
45% of BC consumers say they are willing to pay up to 20% more for products with credible sustainability certifications, compared to a national average of 28%.
Rural Canadian shoppers (populations under 30,000) make 30% fewer shopping trips per month than urban counterparts, but spend 40% more per trip on average.
Brand loyalty in the apparel category has declined 18% among millennials since 2022, driven primarily by cost pressures and expanded access to fast-fashion alternatives.
